governance_context_dimensions.label: Risk / Stakes Level
governance_context_dimensions.description: Consequences of error and required safety margin.

anchors.0: Low stakes; errors are cheap and reversible.
anchors.1: Moderate stakes; errors cost time/money but are manageable.
anchors.2: High stakes; errors have significant external impact; stricter validation required.
anchors.3: Critical; safety/ethics/legal constraints dominate; only conservative routes allowed.

governance_context_dimensions.label: Reversibility Class
governance_context_dimensions.description: How reversible actions and outputs are (especially external side effects).

governance_context_dimensions.label: Permission Flags
governance_context_dimensions.description: Hard constraints on what actions are allowed.

affordance_context_dimensions.label: Budget profile (soft)

values.label: Minimal
values.description: Severely constrained resources; prefer cheap validations and short stages.

values.label: Standard
values.description: Typical research/engineering budget; balanced cost vs evidence.

values.label: Expansive
values.description: Ample resources; can afford stronger validators, sweeps, and redundancy.

affordance_context_dimensions.notes: This is an affordance context (resource regime). It does not encode legal/ethical permissions.

affordance_context_dimensions.label: Budget pressure (dynamic)

anchors.description: Plenty of budget remains; use evidence‑seeking moves freely.
Budget is noticeable; prefer cheap validators; avoid very expensive exploration unless justified.
Budget is tight; prioritize moves with high expected information gain per cost.
Budget nearly exhausted; use minimal must‑gates and stop conditions; consider 'satisficing'.

affordance_context_dimensions.notes: This can change during a run as budget is consumed; record updates per stage if available.

affordance_context_dimensions.label: Tool environment (availability/reliability)
affordance_context_dimensions.description: Availability and reliability of tools/skills (APIs, simulators, compilers, search tools). This is treated as context because it constrains feasibility and expected cost/variance of routes.
affordance_context_dimensions.notes: Affordance context: describes the runtime tool/infra reliability and availability. It is not a 'tension' because it is mostly exogenous; the agent can compensate via redundancy and validator choices.

anchors.description: Environment/data/goal is stable over the planning horizon.
Slow drift; periodic recalibration/monitoring is sufficient.
Drift likely within run; prefer adaptive/evolutionary moves and frequent validation.
Rapid drift or adversarial changes; prefer robust and reversible actions; tighten audit/rollback.

design_notes: Context is partitioned into governance context and affordance context. Governance context expresses normative, permission, safety, audit, and reversibility constraints; affordance context expresses resource, tool, and environmental regimes.

Operational tensions are kept separate because they are endogenous run-state beliefs that may be reduced or shifted by methodological action, whereas governance and affordance contexts are mostly exogenous.
The corresponding instance schemas are published so downstream stage and meta-run records can encode context consistently.

A purely exogenous clarification or retrieval event should usually be logged as provenance or support action inside a stage unless it becomes substantial enough to form its own locally auditable episode.

distinction_rules.summary: Operational tensions are endogenous and reducible by actions; governance/affordance contexts are mostly exogenous and define feasibility/cost regimes.
tension_criteria: Expected to decrease as the agent makes progress (endogenous).
Directly targets the problem structure (ambiguity, evidence gap, inconsistency, etc.).
Can be improved by choosing different methodological moves.
governance_criteria: Normative or permission constraints (safety, legal, audit, reversibility).
Defines hard feasibility gates: actions outside the regime are not allowed.
affordance_criteria: Shapes expected cost/variance and suggests validator profiles, but does not define the task semantics.
